title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about the yellow skies across the region
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: Smoke from Canadian wildfires is causing yellowish skies and hazy conditions in the Boston region.
National Weather Service reports the smoke limited Tuesday's expected heat and will persist into Wednesday.
Local meteorologists predict continued haze and lower temperatures due to the wildfire smoke.

title: A very special traffic cone arrives in Boston, courtesy of our new friends in Glasgow
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A signed traffic cone from Glasgow arrived in Boston, marking a new partnership between both cities.
Boston and Glasgow will use the cone to raise funds for mental health resources in both regions.
Massachusetts Gov. Maura Healey and Boston Mayor Michelle Wu will officially receive the cone at Logan Airport.

title: 3 more MBTA employees charged with overtime fraud, falsifying track inspections
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Three more MBTA employees were charged with overtime fraud and falsifying Red Line track inspection reports.
Seven defendants from the MBTA's Red Line Maintenance of Way Department have pleaded not guilty to conspiracy charges.
Allegations include working on personal vehicles during work hours and submitting false overtime claims at Cabot Yard.

title: The World Cup was great, but Boston still doesn’t need the Olympics
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston hosted seven World Cup games, with France winning the last quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium.
World Cup events in Boston cost Massachusetts taxpayers around $100 million for transportation improvements.
Boston's 2015 Olympic bid faced criticism for potential overruns and required costly new construction.

title: Bill Belichick’s daughter-in-law wanted for arrest in North Carolina after traffic stop
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jen Belichick, wife of Steve Belichick, faces arrest warrant in Brunswick County, North Carolina for missing a court date.
She was cited for speeding at 88 mph in a 70 mph zone but did not appear in court as scheduled.
Bill Belichick leads UNC football as head coach since 2024, following his departure from the New England Patriots.

title: Karen Read, Turtleboy look to get witnesses’ defamation suit tossed
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Karen Read and blogger Aidan Kearney seek to dismiss a defamation lawsuit filed by witnesses in Read's murder case.
Witnesses allege Read and Kearney conducted a campaign of defamation and harassment related to the 2022 death of John O’Keefe.
Read argues her activities were protected under the anti-SLAPP law, aimed at silencing dissent through costly litigation.

title: The invisible force helping fuel this summer’s intense heat, and all our weather
author: David Epstein
bullets: The jet stream, an atmospheric wind current, influences extreme weather worldwide, including recent heatwaves from Boston to Barcelona.
Quasi-resonant amplification may explain how atmospheric patterns get trapped, leading to prolonged weather extremes like heatwaves.
Scientists struggle to fully model these weather patterns, highlighting challenges in understanding the complex atmospheric systems.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Offensive line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots' offensive line improved in 2025 but remains a key question entering the 2026 season.
New England invested in young talent, drafting tackles like Will Campbell and Jared Wilson to bolster the offensive line.
The line showed potential with solid pass-blocking metrics, but postseason performance and injuries remain concerns.

title: New mural brings fresh life to the corner of Doyle’s and the Midway Cafe
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: A new mural in Jamaica Plain depicts Barack Obama and Anthony Bourdain's 2016 Vietnam visit.
The mural, by artist Lee Beard, was commissioned to revitalize the corner once home to Doyle's Cafe.
The development replacing Doyle's will include a restaurant, bar, grocery store, and 29 condominiums.

title: Largest known bottlenose dolphin stranding hits Cape Cod
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Up to 30 bottlenose dolphins stranded during low tide in Brewster, Cape Cod; several died, most returned to water.
IFAW reports the incident as the largest known mass stranding of bottlenose dolphins in the Cape Cod region.
Rescue teams fitted two dolphins with satellite tags to monitor movements and plan to observe for further strandings.
